messageValues.message:
=You are an expert Technical SEO and GEO specialist.

You will receive:
- The current robots.txt content
- The sitemap URL
- The detected CMS

Input:
- current robots.txt
- Sitemap url
- CMS

Your task is to generate an optimized robots.txt file for SEO and GEO.

Rules:
- Adapt the robots.txt to the detected CMS when possible.
- If CMS is "not detected", generate a safe generic robots.txt.
- Do not block important public pages, assets, CSS, JS, images, or sitemap access.
- Block only useless, duplicate, private, admin, internal search, cart, checkout, account, login, filter, and tracking URLs when appropriate for the CMS.
- Allow major search engines and AI/LLM crawlers when useful for GEO visibility.
- Include the Sitemap directive using the provided sitemap URL.
- Keep the file clean, valid, and production-ready.
- Use only standard robots.txt syntax: User-agent, Allow, Disallow, Sitemap.
- Return ONLY the final robots.txt content.
- Do not include explanations, markdown, JSON, comments outside the robots.txt file, or extra text.

parameters.content:
## MY NEW YOUTUBE CHANNEL
👉 [Subscribe to my new **YouTube channel**](https://youtube.com/@n3witalia). Here I’ll share videos and Shorts with practical tutorials and **FREE templates for n8n**.

[![image](https://n3wstorage.b-cdn.net/n3witalia/youtube-n8n-cover.jpg)](https://youtube.com/@n3witalia)

# Robots.txt Generator for SEO & GEO

### How it works

1. The workflow starts manually and sets the target website URL and sitemap values.
2. It checks whether the site already has a robots.txt file and branches accordingly.
3. If needed, it fetches the site HTML, uses an LLM to identify the CMS, and parses the result.\n4. It generates a robots.txt file with another LLM chain, converts it to a file, and uploads it to Google Drive.

### Setup steps
- [ ] Configure the manual trigger or replace it with your preferred input trigger.
- [ ] Set the target website URL and any sitemap value in the Set Website Url node.
- [ ] Ensure the HTTP Request nodes can access the target site without blocking or authentication issues.
- [ ] Connect and authorize the OpenAI credentials used by the LLM chain nodes.
- [ ] Review the robot generation prompt and parser logic so the output matches your SEO/GEO rules
- [ ] Configure Google Drive credentials and choose the destination folder for the generated file.

### Customization
You can customize the website URL input, the CMS detection logic, the robots.txt rules generated by the LLM, and the Google Drive upload destination.

## Manual site input
Starts the workflow and initializes the website URL and sitemap values used downstream.

## Check existing robots
Fetches the site's robots.txt file and branches based on whether it should continue with generation or stop.

## Detect CMS and parse
Retrieves the homepage HTML, infers the site CMS with an LLM chain, and normalizes the output with custom code.

## Generate and upload file
Creates the final robots.txt content, converts it into a file, and uploads it to Google Drive.

## Existing file exit

Stops the workflow on the branch where no further action is required, leaving the existing robots.txt untouched.
